Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Feb-13-2018 Continued To Feb-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement; Petition For Joinder Of Trusts; Petition To Amend Trusts To Conform To Terms Of Settlement
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-300123 The Ernesto & Vangum Moi Revocable Trust and PTR-16-300148 The Ernesto Moi Trust.

Appearances: John Rueppel for Pinky Ho (Petitioner).
James Lamping specially appearing for Attorney Ciarin O'Sullivan on behalf of Noreen Hoi.

The Court noted that there now are consents filed including from the proposed Trustee. Mr. Rueppel stated that he is not aware of any objections, that he filed a declaration regarding the assets, that there is a little over $2,050,000.00, that Mr. O'Sullivan's Client has $100,000.00 in her control (the 1994 Trust), and that both Trusts will continue briefly and should be distributed soon. Mr. Rueppel also stated that the joint assets are being divided by percentages of the agreement and that he does not see any need for separate bonds. The Court granted the Petitions to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. (subject to review and receipt of the orders). Mr. Rueppel is to provide revised orders for both matters indicating bond amounts for each Trust. Said orders are approved as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Continued To Feb-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement; Petition For Joinder Of Trusts; Petition To Amend Trusts To Conform To Terms Of Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Continued To Jan-31-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-300123 The Ernesto & Vangum Moi Trust and
PTR-16-300148 The Ernesto Moi Trust.

Appearances: James O'Neil for Pinky Ho, Yan Liang and The Sharon Chinese Baptist Church.
Ciaran O'Sullivan for Noreen Hoi.

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they are going to amend the petition to no longer seek consolidation and reformation. Mr. O'Sullivan also stated that they are in contact with Almeida Lee, that he hopes to have settlement consent from him, and requested a continuance to January stating that he anticipates wrapping this up shortly. Both Petitions to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. are continued to January 31, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Continued To Dec-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement; Petition For Joinder Of Trusts; Petition To Amend Trusts To Conform To Terms Of Settlement
Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Continued To Dec-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-300123 The Ernesto & Vangum Moi Revocable Trust and PTR-16-300148 The Ernesto Moi Revocable Trust.

Appearances: John Ruppel for Pinky Ho (Petitioner).
Ciarin O'Sullivan for Noreen Hoi.

The Court questions what happened to Almeida's share. Mr. Ruppel stated that the settlement agreement provides that a certain amount will go to Hoi and she will distribute shares to Almeida and the Cousins (to be shared by four people). The Court notes that this is not stated in the settlement agreement and reads language in paragraph j on page three of the agreement. Mr. Ruppel acknowledges that he does not see it either and states that he knows they discussed it. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that Almeida, Jessica and Jerrica did not engage in mediation and have not contributed to the costs of litigation. The Court stated that absent something from Almeida or something from Noreen, this does not seem to be fair. The Court also stated that it wants to know that either Almeida is giving up his claim or not, that part of what Mr. O'Sullivan is asking the Court to do is to reform the Trust and that it is uncomfortable with using Almeida's share to provide for the others. In addition, the Court stated that it usually sees either a petition to approve the settlement agreement or a petition to amend the Trust, and that is does not typically see both. The Petitions to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. are continued to December 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide more information as to why both tracks are appropriate or if the petition is going to be amended. The Court stated that it would like to see the form of the distribution to Noreen.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Continued To Aug-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: (Heard concurrently with Case No. PTR-16-300123)
Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for petitioner Noreen Hoi; Vincent Yu for Pinky Ho.

Mr. O'Sullivan advised the Court that a mediation is scheduled for July 26, 2017, and he requested that the matters be continued to a date after the mediation. The matter was continued to August 15, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court directed counsel to file a report by August 9, 2017 if the matter is resolved, or a joint statement by that date setting forth the issues remaining and proposals for their resolution.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Continued To Jun-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Case PTR-16-300123 heard together with case PTR-16-300148.
Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for petitioner Noreen Hoi; John Rueppel for respondents.

The Court asked for an update on mediation and discovery. Mr. O'Sullivan reported that Mr. Rueppel's clients have indicated they are not interested in mediation. The parties are now in the midst of discovery, with writted discovery requests having been exchanged. He requested a continuance of the hearings on the two petitions for a period of 120 days, to allow time to complete discovery and bring the actions ready for trial. Mr. Rueppel agreed that discovery was now being exchanged and that a continuance was appropriate. Mr. O'Sullivan requested that the Court take action now on his request for issuance of an order prohibiting the use of trust funds to defend his client's petition. The Court stated that he would not issue an interim order restricting the use of trust funds, and the matters were continued to June 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m./1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Demurrer Of Pinky Ho, Yan Ping Liang, And The Sharon Chinese Baptist Church Of San Francisco To Financial Elder Abuse Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Denied

Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Nov-08-2016 Continued To Mar-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard together: PTR-16-300123 The Ernesto & Vangum Moi Revocable Trust and PTR-16-300148 The Ernesto Moi dated 7/24/15 Revocable Trust.
Appearances: Ciaron O'Sullivan for (petitioner) Noreen Hoi. John Rueppel for (trustee) Pinky Ho and (beneficiaries) Yan Ping Liang & Sharon Chinese Baptist Church.

The Court confirmed that the petitions were being opposed, that they will need to be litigated and asked the parties if they have talked to each other. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they just started talking, that a demurrer has been filed for December 15, 2016, that they were waiting for a formal appearance to get going on discovery and that they would like a continuance of 120 days. Mr. Rueppel stated that they are open to ADR. The Court instructed the parties to talk to each other, confirmed that they qualify for Pro Bono Mediation and stated that the court will accommodate that if that is what they want. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they want to be able to make that request at the next hearing. The petition to invalidate purported trust agreements and donative transfers to care to custodian was continued to March 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to contact the court if they want to be referred to pro bono mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition To Invalidate Transported Trust And Donative Transers To Care Of Custodian; For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse, Et Al.
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Continued To Nov-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
